text:
Drug Name: Atorvastatin
Description: Used to lower cholesterol and triglyceride levels.
Side Effects: Muscle pain, nausea, headache
Category: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors, or statins.

Drug Name: Amoxicillin
Description: Antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections.
Side Effects: Diarrhea, rash, nausea.
Category: antibiotics

Drug Name: Lisinopril
Description: Treats high blood pressure and heart failure.
Side Effects: Dizziness, headache, fatigue.
Category: blood pressure

Drug Name: Metformin
Description: Used to control blood sugar levels in type 2 diabetes.
Side Effects: Stomach upset, diarrhea, nausea.
Category:  Diabetes

Drug Name: Omeprazole
Description: Treats gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).
Side Effects: Headache, stomach pain, nausea.
Category: gastroesophageal

Drug Name: Amlodipine
Description: Used to treat high blood pressure and angina.
Side Effects: Swelling, dizziness, fatigue.
Category:  blood pressure

Drug Name: Zolpidem
Description: Treats insomnia.
Side Effects: Drowsiness, dizziness, diarrhea.
Category: Insomnia

Drug Name: Clonazepam
Description: Used to treat seizure and panic disorders.
Side Effects: Drowsiness, dizziness, fatigue.
Category: depression

Drug Name: Montelukast
Description: Used to treat allergies and prevent asthma attacks.
Side Effects: Headache, stomach pain, cough.
Category: allergy

Drug Name: Gabapentin
Description:  Used to treat nerve pain and seizures.
Side Effects: Dizziness, drowsiness, coordination problems.
Category: anticonvulsants

Drug Name: Albuterol
Description: Used to treat bronchospasm.
Side Effects: Nervousness, shaking, headache.
Category: Inhaler

Drug Name: Loratadine
Description: Antihistamine used to relieve allergy symptoms.
Side Effects:  Dry mouth, headache, drowsiness.
Category:  Antihistamine

Drug Name: Ibuprofen
Description: Used to reduce fever and treat pain or inflammation.
Side Effects: Stomach pain, heartburn, nausea.
Category:  inflammatory

Drug Name: Warfarin
Description: Used to prevent blood clots.
Side Effects: Bleeding, bruising, nausea.
Category: blood pressure

Drug Name: Cetirizine
Description: Antihistamine used to treat allergy symptoms.
Side Effects: Drowsiness, dry mouth, fatigue.
Category: Antihistamine

Drug Name: Sertraline
Description: Antidepressant used to treat depression and anxiety.
Side Effects: Nausea, insomnia, dry mouth.
Category: depression

Drug Name: Tramadol
Description:  Used to treat moderate to severe pain.
Side Effects: Dizziness, nausea, constipation.
Category: Pain Killer

Drug Name: Tamsulosin
Description: Used to treat symptoms of an enlarged prostate.
Side Effects: Dizziness, headache, runny nose.
Category:  Gynaecology

Drug Name: Oxycodone
Description: Used to treat moderate to severe pain.
Side Effects: Nausea, drowsiness, constipation.
Category: Pain Killer

Drug Name: Prednisone
Description: Corticosteroid used to treat inflammation.
Side Effects: Insomnia, mood changes, weight gain.
Category: corticosteroid

Drug Name: Fluoxetine
Description:  Antidepressant used to treat depression and OCD.
Side Effects: Nausea, drowsiness, insomnia.
Category:  depression

Drug Name: Metoprolol
Description:  Used to treat high blood pressure and angina.
Side Effects: Dizziness, tiredness, depression.
Category:  blood pressure

Drug Name: Furosemide
Description: Diuretic used to treat fluid retention and swelling.
Side Effects: Dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, dizziness.
Category:  oop diuretics

Drug Name: Duloxetine
Description: Used to treat depression and anxiety.
Side Effects: Nausea, dry mouth, drowsiness.
Category: depression

Drug Name: Simvastatin
Description: Used to lower cholesterol and triglycerides.
Side Effects: Muscle pain, headache, nausea.
Category: cholesterol

Drug Name: Losartan
Description: Used to treat high blood pressure.
Side Effects: Dizziness, back pain, nasal congestion.
Category: blood pressure

Drug Name: Clopidogrel
Description: Used to prevent blood clots.
Side Effects: Bleeding, bruising, headache.
Category: antiplatelet

Drug Name: Hydrocodone
Description: Used to treat severe pain.
Side Effects:  Nausea, drowsiness, constipation.
Category: Pain killer

Drug Name: Trazodone
Description:  Antidepressant used to treat depression and insomnia.
Side Effects:  Drowsiness, dry mouth, dizziness.
Category:  Depression

Drug Name: Ranitidine
Description: Used to reduce stomach acid and treat ulcers.
Side Effects:  Headache, constipation, nausea.
Category:  H2 blockers

Drug Name: Tazarotene
Description: This medication is a retinoid, prescribed for psoriasis and acne. It may decrease skin inflammation and skin changes associated with psoriasis.
Side Effects: Skin: Itching, burning/stinging, redness, skin peeling, irritation, worsening of psoriasis, rash, dry skin, bleeding, localized swelling, high cholesterol levels, desquamation, contact dermatitis, discoloration of skin and photosensitivity.
Category: Dermatology

metadata.consumeType: Adult: Topical- As 0.05 or 0.1% cream/gel: Apply once in the evening.

text:
Drug Name: Isotretinoin 
Description: This medication is a retinoid, prescribed for acne and other skin disorders. It reduces skin oil production, changing the characteristics of the skin oil, and preventing abnormal hardening of the skin.
Side Effects: Allergic reactions, fast heart rate and stroke.
Category: Dermatology

metadata.consumeType: Adult- PO- The recommended dose range is 0.5 to 1.0 mg/kg/day given in two divided doses with food for 15 to 20 weeks.

text:
Drug Name: Minocycline
Description: Minocycline is a broad spectrum tetracycline antibiotic. It acts by inhibiting the growth of bacteria in the body, It may be effective in other infections as well, however several bacteria have developed resistance to the drug, It may be used in patients who are allergic to the penicillin group of drugs as an alternative.
Side Effects: Loss of appetite, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, indigestion, mouth ulcer, discoloration of the tongue and difficulty in swallowing.
Category: Dermatology

metadata.consumeType: Children over 12 years: 50mg every 12 hours, Adults The usual  dosage is 100 mg every 12 hours. The  dosage and duration varies according to the type of infection.For local use in the gums, the dose is 1mg given beneath the gums.

text:
Drug Name: Calamine Lotion
Description: This medication is an anti-itch medication that contains mixture of zinc oxide (ZnO) with about 0.5% iron (III) oxide, prescribed for itching skin conditions. This medication in some cases is used as a mild antiseptic to arrest infections caused by scratching the affected area. Calamine is proved to be an effective medication in acne treatment.
Side Effects: Rash or irritation.
Category: Dermatology

Drug Name: Dimethicone
Description: This medication is an emollient, prescribed for pediculosis, and other skin conditions.
Side Effects: Rarely, redness, pain and irritation at the site of application.
Category: Dermatology

Drug Name: Sucralfate
Description: This medication is a gastric protective agent, prescribed for intestinal ulcers.
Side Effects: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, gastric discomfort, indigestion, flatulence and dry mouth, Itching and rash, Dizziness, sleeplessness, sleepiness and unsteadiness,Back pain and headache.
Category: Dermatology

metadata.consumeType: Adult- PO- The recommended  dosage for duodenal ulcer is 1 g four times per day on an empty stomach.

text:
Drug Name: Adapalene
Description: Adapalene is a topical retinoid-like compound used to treat mild to moderate acne by preventing the formation of pimples acting deeply in the source.It also prevents the formation of new acne by restoring skin texture and tone.
Side Effects: A brief sensation of warmth or stinging may occur immediately after applying the medication, redness, dryness, itching of the skin and scaling, mild burning, or worsening of acne may occur during the first 2-4 weeks of using the medication.
Category: Dermatology

metadata.consumeType: Topical- A thin film of gel should be applied once a day to affected areas after washing the skin.

text:
Drug Name: Nadifloxacin
Description: This medication is a topical antibiotic, prescribed for acne vulgaris. It inhibits the enzyme DNA gyrase that is involved in bacterial replication.
Side Effects: Itching, irritation, redness, flushes, papules, feeling of warmth, increased sweating, contact dermatitis and dryness of the skin.
Category: Dermatology

metadata.consumeType: Topical- Apply a thin layer to the affected area as directed by your physician.

text:
Drug Name: Oxytetracycline
Description: This medication is an antibiotic, prescribed for various infections such as acne, dermatitis, gonorrhea, etc.
Side Effects: Loss of appetite, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, tongue inflammation, difficulty in swallowing and inflammatory lesions.
Category: Dermatology

metadata.consumeType: PO- The recommended dose range is 250 to 1.5gm in divided doses.Topical-Apply a thin layer over the affected skin 4 times per day.

text:
Drug Name: Nadifloxacin
Description: This medication is a topical antibiotic, prescribed for acne vulgaris. It inhibits the enzyme DNA gyrase that is involved in bacterial replication.
Side Effects: Itching, irritation, redness, flushes, papules, feeling of warmth, increased sweating, contact dermatitis and dryness of the skin.
Category: Dermatology

Drug Name: Alogliptin
Description: Alogliptin is prescribed to reduce high blood sugar level in patients with type 2 diabetes along with diet control and exercise.It should not be used in treating diabetic ketoacidosis or type 1 diabetes.  
Side Effects: Upper respiratory tract infection, heart attack, throat inflammation, diarrhea, high blood pressure, headache, back pain and urinary tract infection.
Category: Diabetes

metadata.consumeType: PO - The initial dose is based on patient's condition. Maximum recommended dose is 25/2000mg.

text:
Drug Name: Alogliptin and Metformin
Description: Alogliptin and Metformin combination is used along with diet and exercise to improve blood sugar control or to treat hyperglycemia in patients with type 2 diabetes.High blood sugar level can cause damage to the eyes, kidney, and nerves.This combination medication contains alogliptin, a dipeptidyl-peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitor and metformin, a biguanide.
Side Effects: Lactic Acidosis and pancreatitis.Most Common: Upper respiratory tract infection, nasopharyngitis, diarrhea, hypertension, headache, back pain and urinary tract infection.
Category: Diabetes

metadata.consumeType: (125mg/500mg)- Dose is based on patientâ€™s condition.

text:
Drug Name: Alogliptin and Pioglitazone
Description: Alogliptin and Pioglitazone combination is used to treat high blood sugar level in patients with type 2 diabetes along with diet control and exercise. This combination should not be used to treat type 1 diabetes.This combination medication contains pioglitazone, a thiazolidinedione agent and alogliptin, a DPP-4 inhibitor.
Side Effects: Inflammation of throat and nasal passages, back pain and upper respiratory tract infection.
Category: Diabetes

metadata.consumeType: Dose is based on patientâ€™s condition. Maximum recommended dose is 25/45mg.

text:
Drug Name: Canagliflozin
Description: This medication is an antidiabetic (sodium glucose co-transporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitor), prescribed for type 2 diabetes mellitus along with diet and exercise. For patients with type 2 diabetes along with diabetic kidney disease, physicians should consider using an SGLT2 inhibitor when the eGFR is at or above 30, especially with albuminuria above 300 mg/g, to lower renal and CV risk as per A.D.A recommendations.
Side Effects: Female genital mycotic infections, urinary tract infection and increased urination.
Category: Diabetes

metadata.consumeType: PO- The recommended initial dose is 100 mg once daily.

text:
Drug Name: Diabinese
Description: This medication is a sulfonylurea antidiabetic drug, prescribed for type 2 diabetes. This medication helps to keep blood sugar levels under control.
Side Effects: Body as a Whole : Low blood sugar, Disulfiram-like,Central Nervous System : Dizziness and headache, Gastrointestinal : Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, loss of appetite and hunger, Liver : Jaundice, Skin : Itching and hives, Blood : Anemia and decrease in blood counts.
Category: Diabetes

metadata.consumeType: PO- The recommended initial dose is 250mg/day. It may be adjusted based on patientâ€™s condition.

text:
Drug Name: Colesevelam HCL
Description: This medication is a bile acid sequestrant, prescribed for familial hypercholesterolemia and type 2 diabetes with other medications.
Side Effects: Headache, fatigue, increased level of liver enzyme, runny nose, vomiting, constipation, indigestion, difficulty in swallowinglow blood sugar, nausea and high blood pressure..
Category: Diabetes

metadata.consumeType: For hypercholesterolemia- The recommended dose is one 3.75-gram packet once daily or one 1.875-gram packet twice daily.For Type 2 Diabetes- The recommended dose is 6 tablets once daily or 3 tablets twice daily.

text:
Drug Name: Steglatro
Description: Ertugliflozin tablet is a sodium glucose co-transporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitor which is prescribed for adult patients with type 2 diabetes as an adjunct therapy to diet control and exercise with a focus to improve glycemic control.It is prescribed either as a monotherapy or in combination with metformin or sitagliptin.  
Side Effects: Common: Frequent urination, low blood sugar level, genital fungal infections, Gastrointestinal: Nausea, pain in the stomach, vomiting, Cardiovascular: Low blood volume, low blood pressure, faster heartbeat, increased LDL-cholesterol level in blood, Nervous system: Confusion, dizziness, headache, irritability, weakness, Respiratory: Difficulty in breathing, nasopharyngitis, Kidney: Urinary tract infections (pyelonephritis, urosepsis or bladder infections), pain or burning sensation while passing urine, blood in urine, reduced kidney function, Others: Foot ulcers or sores, fever, pain or tenderness in the lower limbs, pain in the back, reduced weight, increased thirst
Category: Diabetes

metadata.consumeType: The recommended initial dose of ertugliflozin tablets is 5 mg to be taken once a day.The dose of ertugliflozin can be increased to 15 mg once daily to achieve adequate glycemic control

text:
Drug Name: Byetta
Description: This medication is a incretin mimetic, prescribed for type 2 diabetes with diet and exercise, either alone or with other medications. For patients with chronic kidney disease who are at elevated risk for CV events, a glucagon-like peptide 1 receptor agonist may lower risk for albuminuria progression and/or CV events, according to new (2019)recommendations of The American Diabetes Association It works by stimulating the pancreas to secrete insulin in hyperglycemic condition.  
Side Effects: Most Common: Nausea.Central Nervous System: Dizziness or lightheadedness, nervousness, mood changes, weakness, shakiness, confusion, epilepsy and loss of consciousness.Gastrointestinal: Extreme thirst, extreme hunger, stomach upset, vomiting, diarrhea and difficulty in swallowing. Skin: Rash, hives and itching. Allergic reactions: Swelling of the face, throat, tongue, lips, eyes, hands, feet, ankles, or lower legs and difficulty in breathing.
Category: Diabetes

metadata.consumeType: Adult- The initial dose is 5 mcg injected under the skin (subcutaneously) twice daily, 60 minutes before breakfast or dinner.

text:
Drug Name: Glibenclamide
Description: TGlibenclamide is an oral hypoglycemic (glucose lowering) drug used to control blood sugar levels in patients with type 2 diabetes in addition to diet and exercise.It belongs to the chemical group of sulfonylureas
Side Effects: Digestive tract: Nausea, vomiting, flatulence, abdominal fullness, diarrhea, constipation, heartburn, liver dysfunction, Skin: Rash, skin swelling, photosensitivity reactions (skin reactions following exposure to sunlight) including porphyria cutanea tarda (which is associated with an enzyme deficiency, Blood: Low blood counts, low blood glucose levels, low blood sodium levels, Others: Tiredness, weakness, fever, other allergic reactions, weight gain, joint or muscle pain.
Category: Diabetes

metadata.consumeType: The treatment is started with a low dose of 2.5 mg per day, and increased up to a maximum of 20 mg per day according to the blood glucose levels.

text:
Drug Name: Ademetionine
Description: Ademetionine is used for treating chronic liver disease such as intrahepatic cholestasis.
Side Effects: Nervous system disorders: Headache, insomnia, dizziness, nervousness
Category: Depression

Drug Name: Alprazolam
Description: This medication is a benzodiazepine, prescribed for anxiety and panic disorders.
Side Effects: Gastrointestinal : Dry mouth, constipation, diarrhea, nausea/vomiting and increased salivation.
Category: Depression

metadata.consumeType: The starting dose for treating anxiety is 0.25-0.5 mg, 3 times daily using immediate release tablets.

text:
Drug Name: Amitriptyline
Description: This medication is a tricyclic antidepressant, prescribed for depression.
Side Effects: Allergic : Skin rash, hives, photosensitivity, swelling of the face and tongue.
Category: Depression

metadata.consumeType: Adult: PO- Depression- Initial:50-75 mg/day; up to 150 mg/day if needed.

text:
Drug Name: Chlorhexidine
Description: This medication is a chemical antiseptic, prescribed for gingivitis, cleansing skin and wound areas.
Side Effects: Skin sensitivity, irritation of eye, mucosal irritation and staining of the teeth and tongue.
Category: Dental

metadata.consumeType: As 0.2% solution: Rinse with 10 ml for 1 min 2-3 times/day.

text:
Drug Name: Feracrylum
Description: Feracrylum is used as a hemostatic (to stop bleeding) and an anti-septic for the management of post-operative wounds, cuts.
Side Effects: Gastrointestinal: Nausea, stomach pain.
Category: Dental

metadata.consumeType: Feracrylum is available as a 1% sterile solution (1% w/v feracrylum), as 50 and 15 gm gel tubes (1% w/w feracrylum) and tulle ( 3% w/v feracrylum).

text:
Drug Name: Gensparin
Description: Gensparin prevents the formation of blood clots and is hence used for the treatment of thrombolytic disorders.
Side Effects:  Skin: Rashes, pale skin, clots at the puncture site.
Category: Dental

metadata.consumeType: The recommended dose of the drug is 20mg/0.2ml injection.
